steps.title: Identify the conflict between prediction and result
steps.content: The theory posits that a habitat's initial species diversity determines its eventual diversity. Cáceres' research directly contradicts this: pools starting with diverse zooplankton and pools starting with a single species ended up equally diverse—the initial condition had no lasting impact.

steps.title: Match the researchers' assessment
steps.content: C directly expresses what the researchers would conclude: the theory appears logical (“may seem plausible”) but the data contradicts it (“not supported by the research team's findings”). A overstates the theory's correctness despite the failed prediction, B ignores the evidence entirely, and D misinterprets refutation as a conditional limitation. That matches choice C — C is correct.
